summary:
An aspiration of a keratocyst typically yields fluid rich in proteins because it contains large quantities of keratin; thus, option B correctly identifies its characteristic feature as having high soluble protein content. This assessment is strongly backed by multiple reliable medical resources indicating abundant keratin present inside such cystic formations contributing significantly to overall solubility properties observed upon extraction or analysis. Therefore, maintaining original choice firmly at hand,
\boxed{B}.
The aspirate from a keratocyst has high soluble protein content mainly due to extensive keratin presence, confirmed through various studies emphasizing the unique characteristics of keratocysts containing considerable amounts of keratin-derived substances during aspirations. Consistent with previous evaluations based largely on keratin’s role, retaining selection for
\boxed{B}.
